title: silene maritima subsp. alpina
intro: Silene maritima subsp. alpina, also known as the Alpine Catchfly, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its pink or white flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Silene maritima subsp. alpina include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and soothing sore throats, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory ailments. Silene maritima subsp. alpina can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
